name: ntfy
instructions:
You are connected to the 'ntfy' host via rrsh, a read-only diagnostic gateway. Call list_commands first — the args_pattern regex on each rule tells you exactly which argv shapes are accepted. Rules whose 'as' field contains 'root' auto-elevate (implicit sudo) — you do NOT need to pass {"as":"root"} for those.

LOG LAYOUT on this host:
  /var/log/ntfy.log                    — main ntfy server log (current)
  /var/log/ntfy.log.N.gz               — daily rotations, all gzipped (logrotate has no delaycompress for ntfy)
  /var/log/ntfy-goroutine.log          — periodic goroutine dumps written by emergency-restart cron (every 2 min)
  /var/log/ntfy-health-check.log       — synthetic health-check probe output (cron, every 2 min)
  /var/log/ntfy-database-backup.log    — hourly backup script output
  /var/log/nginx/access.log            — 2xx/3xx HTTP responses
  /var/log/nginx/access.log.1          — yesterday's, uncompressed; .2.gz onwards are gzipped
  /var/log/nginx/access_error.log      — 4xx/5xx HTTP responses (scan/attack noise lands here)
  /var/log/nginx/access_error.log.N    — same rotation pattern (.1 uncompressed, .N.gz compressed)
  /var/log/nginx/error.log             — nginx's own error log + rotations
  /var/log/auth.log                    — SSH attempts, sudo activity, login session events
  /var/log/syslog                      — general system log

For uncompressed .log and .log.1 files, use cat/tail/head/grep/wc. For gzipped rotations (*.gz), use zcat or zgrep — ztail and zhead do NOT exist on this system, so use a pipeline like `zcat /var/log/ntfy.log.5.gz | tail -n 200` when you need the last N lines of a compressed rotation.

For systemd unit logs (live, in-memory journal), use journalctl with -u {ntfy,nginx,ssh,sshd}. systemctl status / is-active / show inspect unit state.

fail2ban-client status shows active jails and currently-banned IPs. lastb shows failed SSH attempts, last shows successful logins.

No write access. If a command you want isn't allowed, stop and tell the operator rather than trying to work around the matcher.

commands.description: Show the effective username.
Show the user/group identity (uid, gid, groups).
Print the system hostname.
Show how long the system has been up and the load average.
Print kernel/OS info. Only `-a` is permitted.
List processes. Permitted args: `aux`, `auxf`, `-ef`, or `-eo <format>`.
Take one batch-mode snapshot of top (`-bn1`).
Show the process tree.
Report memory usage. Optional `-h`, `-m`, or `-g`; no args also OK.
Report filesystem space usage. Optional `-h` or `-i`; no args also OK.
Summarize disk usage under /var/log (or a subpath of it). Must be `-sh <path>`.
List block devices.
Show network state. Permitted: `addr`, `route`, `link`, `link show`.
List listening sockets with PIDs. Permitted: `-tlnp`, `-ulnp`, `-tulnp`.
Ping a host a fixed number of times. Must be `-c <count> <host>`.
Perform DNS lookups.

Fetch the Prometheus node_exporter metrics endpoint. Must be `-sS http://<host-10.x-ip>:9090/metrics`. The metrics endpoint is bound to the host's private 10.x interface (NOT localhost), so use `ip addr` first to discover the right address, then pass it here.
Show recent successful logins from /var/log/wtmp. Optional `-n <count>` or `-<count>`.
Show recent FAILED login attempts from /var/log/btmp. Useful for spotting SSH brute-force activity. Optional `-n <count>` or `-<count>`.
Inspect fail2ban state. `status` lists active jails; `status <jail>` (e.g. `status sshd` or `status nginx-400`) shows currently-banned IPs. Jail names may contain hyphens.
Read systemd journal for ntfy, nginx, ssh, or sshd. Patterns: `[--no-pager] -u <unit> [-n <count> | --since <expr>]`. Auto-elevated so journal entries from other users are visible.

Tail the last N lines of an uncompressed log file. Covers /var/log/ntfy*.log, /var/log/nginx/*.log, /var/log/syslog, /var/log/auth.log, plus their .1 (uncompressed) rotations. For gzipped rotations (*.gz) use zcat | tail instead.
Read the first N lines of an uncompressed log file. Same path coverage as tail.
Dump an uncompressed log file. Same path coverage as tail. For gzipped rotations use zcat.
Dump a gzipped log rotation (*.log.N.gz). Pipe through tail/head for the last/first N lines: `zcat /var/log/ntfy.log.5.gz | tail -n 200`.
List /var/log or a subpath. Must be `-l[a][h] <path>`.
Count lines in an uncompressed log file. Same path coverage as tail.
Case-insensitive grep against an uncompressed log file. Same path coverage as tail. Must be `-i <pattern> <path>`. For gzipped rotations use zgrep.
Case-insensitive grep against a gzipped log rotation (*.log.N.gz). Must be `-i <pattern> <path>`.

commands.args: ^(status ntfy|status nginx|is-active ntfy|is-active nginx|list-timers|show ntfy|show nginx)$
commands.description: Inspect systemd units. Permitted: `status|is-active|show <ntfy|nginx>`, or `list-timers`.
Inspect ntfy state. Permitted: `user list`, `access list`, `tier list`.
